name: Can I sue my boss if I got hurt on the job in Boise?
description: Workers' comp is usually your only remedy; you can't sue your employer unless intentional misconduct.[5]

mainEntity.name: Can I sue my boss if I got hurt on the job in Boise?

acceptedAnswer.text: Workers' comp is usually your only remedy; you can't sue your employer unless intentional misconduct.[5]
acceptedAnswer.description:
**No, in Idaho and Utah, workers' compensation is generally your exclusive remedy against your employer for on-the-job injuries.**

Workers' comp laws provide no-fault benefits like medical care and lost wages in exchange for immunity from lawsuits.[5] Exceptions exist for intentional harm or gross negligence by the employer.

- Third parties (e.g., equipment manufacturers) may be suable separately.
- Prove employer intent or removed immunity to pursue a lawsuit.
- Benefits cover 66-75% of wages plus medical costs.

Document everything and file promptly. An attorney can assess if exceptions apply in your case.
